software_item.name: interviewer4
software_item.description: Interview and questionnaire service responsible for collecting patient symptoms through guided questionnaires, executing branching logic, and managing interview state and answers.
software_item.safety_class_rationale: Class B: Software that can contribute to hazardous situations but is not the sole cause of harm. Collects clinical data (symptoms, medical history) that drives downstream triage decisions but does not directly determine clinical outcomes. Incorrect data collection or interview logic errors could lead to wrong information being passed to the rule engine, potentially affecting care recommendations.

hazard_contributions.description: Interview flow logic error causes questions to be skipped or shown out of order
hazard_contributions.cause: Bug in QuestionnaireExecutor state machine, incorrect condition evaluation, or goto model processing failure
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient fails to report critical symptoms leading to incorrect triage level or missed urgent conditions

hazard_contributions.description: Patient answers are lost or corrupted during save to database
hazard_contributions.cause: Database transaction failure, JPA entity mapping error, JSON serialization failure of AnswerValue, or concurrent modification during answer filtering
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Clinical data loss results in incomplete patient assessment and potentially unsafe triage decisions

hazard_contributions.description: Answer filtering logic removes valid answers when patient changes previous response
hazard_contributions.cause: Bug in filterAnswers() algorithm (lines 93-136) that incorrectly identifies answers to remove when detecting duplicates
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient's symptom data is incomplete, leading to under-triage or failure to identify red flag symptoms

hazard_contributions.description: Interview state becomes inconsistent between database and in-memory representation
hazard_contributions.cause: Race condition during concurrent access, cache invalidation failure, or transaction boundary issues
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient sees stale or incorrect questions, answers are associated with wrong questions, or interview cannot be completed

hazard_contributions.description: External data import failure prevents interview from loading required clinical context
hazard_contributions.cause: HealthData service unavailable, incorrect patient ID mapping, or missing required import data
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Interview proceeds without patient's medical history (medications, conditions), leading to incomplete assessment

hazard_contributions.description: Interview data passed to rule engine is incomplete or malformed
hazard_contributions.cause: Serialization error during result export, missing required fields in InterviewResult, or incorrect answer value mapping
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Rule engine receives incomplete data and produces incorrect triage recommendation

hazard_contributions.description: Access control bypass allows unauthorized access to patient interview data
hazard_contributions.cause: Failure in InterviewAccessEnforcer validation, JWT authentication bypass, or patient-practitioner relationship check failure
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Privacy breach, GDPR violation, or malicious actor views/modifies patient health data

hazard_contributions.description: Interview expires or is locked prematurely, preventing patient from completing symptom assessment
hazard_contributions.cause: Incorrect expiry time calculation, race condition in lock operation, or scheduled expiration job running while patient is active
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient unable to complete triage, forced to restart interview, potential delay in care

hazard_contributions.description: File upload handling allows malicious files or exceeds storage limits
hazard_contributions.cause: Insufficient validation in FileService, object storage integration failure, or missing file type checks
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: System compromise via malicious file, denial of service via storage exhaustion, or patient photos lost

hazard_contributions.description: Null answer value causes NullPointerException in answer processing
hazard_contributions.cause: Missing null check in getAnswerValue method when AnswerValue is null
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Interview crashes, patient loses progress, must restart symptom assessment

risk_controls_implemented.description: Answer timestamp and index-based ordering ensures chronological consistency
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Answer entity has auto-generated index (database sequence) and timestamp. Answers are sorted by index first, then timestamp, ensuring deterministic ordering even during concurrent modifications.

risk_controls_implemented.description: Database transaction management ensures atomic answer saves
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Spring @Transactional annotations on service methods ensure answers are saved atomically with interview state updates. Rollback on exception prevents partial saves.

risk_controls_implemented.description: Interview access control enforces patient-practitioner relationship checks
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: InterviewAccessEnforcer validates JWT user role and patient relationship before allowing interview access. Separate endpoints for ROLE_PATIENT, ROLE_PRACTITIONER, ROLE_SYSTEM with @Secured annotations.

risk_controls_implemented.description: Required import validation throws IllegalStateException when critical data is missing
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: loadImportedData method checks if ImportDataModel.isRequired() and throws exception if required import fails. Interview cannot proceed without required external data.

risk_controls_implemented.description: Questionnaire validation ensures valid questionnaire IDs before interview creation
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: assertQuestionnaires method validates all questionnaire IDs exist in code24Registry before creating interview. Throws BAD_REQUEST if questionnaire not found.

risk_controls_implemented.description: Interview locking mechanism prevents concurrent modifications
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: lockedAt timestamp prevents modifications after interview is locked. Lock operation is idempotent and updates lockedAt in single transaction.

risk_controls_implemented.description: Audit logging of interview events for traceability
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: InterviewerEventSender publishes interview events (created, answered, locked, cancelled) to JMS topic for audit trail and downstream processing.

risk_controls_implemented.description: Exception handling and logging for questionnaire execution failures
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Try-catch blocks in loadImportedData log failures. QuestionnaireExecutor logs warnings when questions not found. Service methods throw InterviewerException with error types.

risk_controls_implemented.description: Database connection pool health checks and monitoring
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Hikari connection pool configured with validation queries (SELECT 1), minimum idle connections, and health check endpoints exposed via Spring Actuator.

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Clinical validation of questionnaire content and branching logic
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Software item cannot validate clinical appropriateness of questions or symptom assessment logic - this requires clinical expertise and ongoing review

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Monitoring and alerting for interview completion rates and failure patterns
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Item-level logging exists but system-level aggregation and alerting required to detect patterns indicating hazardous conditions

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: User training on interview cancellation and restart procedures
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Software cannot force users to follow proper procedures when technical issues occur

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: System-level data integrity validation between interviewer and rule engine
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Interviewer cannot validate downstream consumption of interview results - requires cross-service validation

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Backup and recovery procedures for database failures
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Item relies on MySQL database but cannot implement backup/recovery strategy - requires infrastructure-level controls

failure_modes.name: Questionnaire executor state machine failure
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: getCurrentQuestion returns wrong question or empty, interview flow breaks, patient stuck or gets irrelevant questions
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient cannot complete triage, incomplete symptom data sent to rule engine, potential under-triage
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Unit tests for state transitions, integration tests for questionnaire flows, user reports of incorrect questions
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Patient must cancel and restart interview, no automatic recovery implemented

failure_modes.name: Answer persistence transaction rollback
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Answer not saved to database, transaction rolls back, previous answers retained
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient sees previous answer, may re-answer and succeed, or interview appears stuck if repeated failures
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Transaction exception logged, HTTP 500 error returned to client, database integrity constraints enforced
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: User retries answer submission, Spring retry mechanism on transient failures

failure_modes.name: External service unavailable (HealthData, Directory2)
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Import data fails, REST client throws exception, interview creation may fail if required data missing
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Interview proceeds without patient medical history if import not required, or fails completely if required import unavailable
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: HTTP client timeout or connection exception, logged and potentially thrown as InterviewerException
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Required imports fail fast with IllegalStateException, optional imports logged and skipped, manual retry required

failure_modes.name: Database connection pool exhaustion
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: All database operations fail, service unavailable, HTTP 500 responses
failure_modes.effect_on_system: No new interviews can be created, existing interviews cannot be accessed or updated, system-wide outage for interviewer
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Connection timeout exception after 5 seconds, Hikari metrics exposed via Actuator, alerts triggered
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Connection pool recovers when connections released, may require service restart if pool corrupted, monitoring alerts ops team

failure_modes.name: JMS message delivery failure for interview events
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Event not published to topic, downstream services miss interview state changes, audit trail incomplete
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Other services (actions module) may not be notified of interview completion, potential delay in care workflow
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: JMS exception logged, message may be retried by Artemis, DLQ monitoring for persistent failures
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Artemis message redelivery on transient failures, DLQ for poison messages, manual replay required for lost events

failure_modes.name: Concurrent interview modification race condition
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Two users modify interview simultaneously, last write wins, potential lost updates or inconsistent state
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient or practitioner sees stale data, answers may be overwritten, lock operation may fail
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Optimistic locking exception from JPA, logged but may not be visible to user
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: User must refresh and retry, no automatic conflict resolution

failure_modes.name: JWT authentication token expired or invalid
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: All API calls return HTTP 401 Unauthorized, user cannot access interview
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient must re-authenticate, interview progress preserved if session token refreshed, data loss if user abandons
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: JWT validation filter rejects request, HTTP 401 response, logged
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Client must obtain new JWT token, interview state preserved in database, user can resume after re-auth

failure_modes.name: Answer filtering algorithm removes wrong answers
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Valid answers removed from answer list, patient symptom data incomplete
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Interview results missing critical answers, rule engine receives incomplete data, potential under-triage
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Unit tests for filtering logic, user reports of missing answers, discrepancy between UI and stored answers
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: No automatic recovery, patient must re-answer questions if detected, data may be permanently lost

failure_modes.name: Interview expiration during active session
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Interview marked expired while patient is actively answering, subsequent answers fail
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient forced to restart interview, progress lost, frustration and potential abandonment
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Scheduled job logs expired interviews, patient receives error on next answer attempt
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Patient must create new interview and restart, no way to extend expired interview

failure_modes.name: Null AnswerValue causes exception
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: NullPointerException thrown, transaction rolls back, answer not processed
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient sees error, must retry answer, repeated failures block interview progress
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Exception logged, HTTP 500 error, test coverage detects null handling
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: User retries with valid answer, null check added at method entry (line 197-199)

failure_modes.name: Object storage unavailable for file uploads
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: File upload fails, exception thrown, patient cannot attach photos to interview
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Interview can proceed without file if not required, visual symptom documentation lost if file required
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Object storage client exception, logged, HTTP error returned to client
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: User retries upload when storage available, interview can be locked without file if optional

failure_modes.name: Questionnaire cache returns stale version
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Interview uses outdated questionnaire logic, wrong questions shown, incorrect branching
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patients assessed with obsolete clinical logic, potential safety issue if critical changes not reflected
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Version mismatch logged, clinical review detects outdated logic, cache TTL monitoring
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Cache invalidation forces reload, service restart clears cache, interviews in progress may complete with old version

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: All interview and answer data stored here. Database failure = complete data loss for active interviews. Corruption = incorrect triage data.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Connection pool retries (5s timeout), validation queries, transaction rollback on failure. No data recovery mechanism beyond database backups.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.timeout: 5000ms connection timeout, configured in Hikari pool
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: Hikari connection pool retries, no application-level retry

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Provides patient medical history (medications, conditions, allergies) required for safe triage. Failure means interview proceeds without critical context.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Required imports throw IllegalStateException and block interview. Optional imports logged and skipped. No retry mechanism.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.timeout: Not explicitly configured, likely default HTTP client timeout

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Validates patient identity and user-patient relationships. Failure could block access to interview or allow unauthorized access.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Service throws exception if patient/user not found. No fallback mechanism. Access denied on failure.

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Publishes interview lifecycle events to downstream services (actions module). Failure means other services unaware of interview completion, delaying care workflow.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: JMS exception logged but does not block interview operations. Message may be retried by Artemis. DLQ for poison messages.

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Stores patient-uploaded files (photos of symptoms). Failure prevents file upload but should not block interview if files optional.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Exception on upload failure, logged, returned to client. No retry. Interview can proceed without file if not required.

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Maps patient symptom text to medical condition codes for questionnaire selection. Failure means patient cannot start triage from free-text symptom.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Timeout configured (readTimeout, connectionTimeout). Exception logged and returned to client. User must retry or use condition picker.

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: CRITICAL: Exports completed interview data to rule engine for triage decision. Malformed or incomplete data causes incorrect triage level, potential patient harm.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Serialization errors logged. No validation that downstream received data correctly. Assumes synchronous call succeeds.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: None - one-shot export on interview lock

verification_gaps.description: Answer filtering algorithm lacks comprehensive test coverage for edge cases (simultaneous duplicates, out-of-order indices)
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add parameterized tests for filterAnswers() covering: multiple duplicate chains, null timestamps, equal timestamps with different indices, concurrent modifications

verification_gaps.description: No integration test verifying interview result data integrity when exported to rule engine
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add end-to-end test that creates interview, answers questions, locks, and validates exported JSON matches expected schema and contains all answers

verification_gaps.description: No test coverage for database transaction rollback scenarios during answer save
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add test that simulates transaction failure (constraint violation, deadlock) and verifies rollback leaves interview in consistent state

verification_gaps.description: HealthData service failure handling not fully tested - unclear if required vs optional imports properly enforced
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add integration tests with mock HealthData service returning errors, verify required imports fail fast and optional imports skip gracefully

verification_gaps.description: No test for concurrent interview lock operations from different users
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add multi-threaded test attempting concurrent locks on same interview, verify only one succeeds and state remains consistent

verification_gaps.description: Interview expiration logic not tested for edge case where patient is actively answering when expiration job runs
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add test that simulates active interview being expired, verify patient sees appropriate error and can recover

verification_gaps.description: JMS event publishing failure handling not tested - unclear if failed events are retried or lost
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add integration test with mock JMS broker that fails, verify event retry behavior and DLQ handling

cross_item_interfaces: Interview result export to rule-engine-module (CRITICAL for triage decision)
HealthData import from healthdata service (required for medical history)
JMS events to actions-module for workflow coordination
Object storage for patient file uploads
shared_hazards: Data integrity failures exist across all items that persist clinical data
External service unavailability affects interviewer4, actions-module, rule-engine-module
Authentication/authorization bypass risk exists in all patient-facing services
Database connection pool exhaustion could affect multiple services sharing database
system_level_controls_needed: End-to-end data integrity validation from patient input through triage decision
System-wide monitoring for interview completion rates and failure patterns
Cross-service transaction coordination if interview affects multiple services
Clinical validation and versioning of questionnaire content
Disaster recovery and backup strategy for patient interview data
